key_insight: A symmetric dual-chip piezoelectric strain cell can deliver homogeneous uniaxial strain to thick, square quantum-device substrates while suppressing bending and shear.
engineering_relevance: The design enables practical strain tuning of semiconductor qubits by combining high-density RF/DC access with electrical isolation from high-voltage actuators.
method: Use a highly symmetric dual-chip loading geometry to balance forces on the substrate.
Integrate a high-density RF/DC interposer for standard wire bonding and device connectivity.
Enclose the piezo actuators in a grounded Faraday cage to prevent stray electrostatic effects.
evidence_signal: Finite element simulations and surface strain measurements on a 200 μm silicon die show 215 με strain at 200 V piezo bias.
watch_item: Performance depends on precise symmetric mounting and robust isolation; misalignment or wiring/packaging leakage could reintroduce flexure or unwanted Stark shifts.
paper_title: A Cryogenic Uniaxial Strain Cell for Quantum Devices
